content: We can adapt the intrinsic reward design of the 2025 ICM curiosity mechanism to reframe the LOCOMO long-term conversational memory benchmark: instead of only testing whether an LLM agent remembers past conversation details, we reward agents for actively exploring and encoding novel user preferences (analogous to the diverse snack menu offerings of the physical Locomo food venue) to build more useful long-term memory. This creates a new reinforcement learning training pipeline that incentivizes agents to prioritize retention of unexpected, personally novel user information over generic conversation facts.
